title: NYC DOT — Shared Definitions
description: Reusable JSON Schema definitions shared across NYC Department of Transportation object schemas. Reconciles fields common to nyc.gov/dot, nycstreetdesign.info (Drupal), and the DOT NYC Open Data (Socrata) datasets — most importantly the LION street SegmentReference that DOT uses to key almost every street asset.

x-nyc-commons.note: Geography/identifier/address/money definitions are $ref'd from the canonical nyc-commons set; agency-specific definitions remain local.

Borough.$comment: Canonical definition provided by nyc-commons (geography.json#/$defs/Borough); migrated from a local definition. Back-compatible: the $defs name is unchanged, so object schemas referencing _common.json#/$defs/Borough still resolve.

Coordinates.$comment: Canonical definition provided by nyc-commons (geography.json#/$defs/Coordinates); migrated from a local definition. Back-compatible: the $defs name is unchanged, so object schemas referencing _common.json#/$defs/Coordinates still resolve.

Geography.$comment: Canonical definition provided by nyc-commons (geography.json#/$defs/GeographySpine); migrated from a local definition. Back-compatible: the $defs name is unchanged, so object schemas referencing _common.json#/$defs/Geography still resolve.

SegmentReference.description: A reference to a LION street segment — the DOT join key. Open Data carries this as SegmentID / segmentid on nearly every street asset (bike routes, traffic counts, closures), plus the on/from/to street naming used to describe a blockface.

segmentId.description: LION SegmentID (Open Data SegmentID / segmentid) — the citywide street-centerline key.

onStreet.description: The street the asset is on (Open Data OnStreetName / on_street / street).

fromStreet.description: Cross street the segment runs from (FromStreetName / from_street).

toStreet.description: Cross street the segment runs to (ToStreetName / to_street).

sideOfStreet.description: Side of street (Open Data side_of_street) where relevant.

GeoJSONGeometry.description: A GeoJSON geometry. DOT street assets are overwhelmingly geospatial; Open Data stores them as the_geom (Point / MultiLineString / MultiPolygon) or WKT — normalized to GeoJSON here.
